title: pedicularis pacifica
intro: Pedicularis pacifica, also known as Pacific lousewort or Pacific pedicularis, is a flowering plant species native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It belongs to the borage family and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health. Pedicularis pacifica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
